text:
Masadora × Big Strides × Bomber (マサドラ×躍進×, Masadora × Yakushin × Bomā) is the 79 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 1 st episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on February 18 th , 2004.

Overview

Biscuit teaches Gon and Killua several Nen techniques while they train in the badlands. The Phantom Troupe gets kicked off Greed Island. Genthru exposes himself as the Bomber.

Synopsis

Biscuit, Gon, and Killua continue through the badlands on their way to Masadora. Biscuit continues to test their use of Gyo along the way, forcing them to do copious amounts of push-ups if they respond too slowly. They pass through a deserted village and run along a stretch of beach on their way.

When they finally arrive in the strange city, Biscuit temporarily goes off alone, and the boys wait for her. Biscuit returns with her hair fixed up in a high ponytail to cover the uneven haircut she received from Binolt's attack; Gon compliments her on her new look. Although the boys are eager to purchase spell cards of their own, Biscuit has them search for a department store first. While she's busy looking around for supplies, Gon and Killua get directions on where to buy spell cards, but when they meet up with Biscuit to tell her, she asks if they are tired, and when they tell her they still have a lot of energy, she makes them go back out to train for another two and a half hours instead.

After returning to the badlands again, Biscuit uses some of the item cards she picked up at the department store to generate some digging equipment, telling the boys they'll return to Masadora now. Still, this time, they must go directly through the mountains by digging through themselves. Although initially shocked, the boys quickly get busy without much complaint. However, shoveling through one mountain thoroughly strains their bodies; despite only moving about two hundred meters, they collapse onto the ground upon reaching the other side.

Biscuit makes them rest holding two long poles of rope with big rocks attached at the top, saying that is how they will sleep from then on; if they let go of the rope poles, they will get crushed by the rocks. Biscuit also says she'll occasionally throw knives at the ropes to cut them, which they'll have to avoid, so they'll need to stay alert even while they're resting. Initially, Gon is worried about the idea, but Killua has no trouble resting, as he remembers when his father used the same training method with him. At night, Gon accidentally lets go of the rope in his sleep, and his rock hits him on the head. Biscuit makes him put the rock back himself before continuing. This happens several times through the night, leaving Gon worn down by the next day, while Killua is fairly well rested. Biscuit gives them a bigger rock each night.

One day, Gon hits a particularly hard rock section while digging, and he can't get through with his shovel alone. Gon considers using his Nen to punch through, but instead realizes that his Nen also encompasses his shovel when he's holding it. Gon and Killua can then use their Nen to make digging a lot easier for themselves. Biscuit is proud of them for finally discovering Shu, but also knows they will wear themselves out much quicker while using it if they are not careful. Just like she predicted, Gon and Killua exhaust themselves not long after.

Eventually, the boys can work much quicker through the mountains, and Gon also significantly improves at holding up his rock in his sleep; when Biscuit throws a knife to cut Gon's rope, he is easily able to dodge out of the way to avoid getting hit by his rock.

The boys are excited to finally reach Masadora, but as soon as they get there, Biscuit tells them they'll be returning to the badlands. This time, however, the boys are instructed to collect monster cards by defeating the monsters they encounter. She cringes at some of their poor attempts to catch monsters, but doesn't say anything to them about it. She's also impressed by how well Greed Island is programmed to help players improve and wonders if maybe Ging worked on this project to train his son. Although it takes them some time, the boys can eventually get cards of monsters they were previously unable to defeat, like the Bubble Horse.

Biscuit switches over to training the boys in defense, starting with using Ko to strengthen her attacks while making the boys block her attacks without allowing them to dodge. She teaches the boys how to use Ken to defend against her. Despite attacking very slowly, Biscuit's hit still causes Gon to fly backward, much to Gon and Killua's shock. She tries to get him to defend against her again, but using Ken tires Gon out too much, and he falls to his knees.

Over the course of a month, Gon and Killua continue to train by digging and practicing to sustain their Ken for longer periods of time. By the end, Gon still gets knocked back by Biscuit's attack, but is able to remain on his feet and sustain Ken for a full thirty minutes.

Biscuit then has the boys train in combat against each other, focusing on using Ryu. Afterward, Biscuit allows them a break while she explains the final stage of their training. She gets them to train for Enhancement skills, having Gon and Killua break a thousand rocks using one single rock of their own. Neither make it to two hundred before their own rocks break. Killua gets bored easily and wants to move on to Transmutation training, but is told to wait for another day. Gon and Killua also get into a rock-paper-scissors match, and Biscuit goes into a bit of the history of martial arts, as she claims rock-paper-scissors originated from martial arts. With this, Gon starts to understand what he wants his Nen ability to be and works on developing his technique Rock.

The small group of Phantom Troupe members traversing through Greed Island manage to get to the island through alternative methods to prove their theory that Greed Island exists in the real world. Razor appears, approaching their group to discuss their trespassing. Razor introduces himself as one of the game's creators and tells them he has to kick them out for trespassing. Although angry about it, Phinks gladly accepts Razor's invitation to come back and fight him if they enter the game through legitimate means, reminding himself that Razor appears to be very strong. Razor expels the group using the card Eliminate, and they're teleported elsewhere. He also serves a ball of energy in the shape of a volleyball at a ship idling in the ocean nearby, destroying it.

At Nickes' Alliance's hideout, Nickes announces to the members of his group that they have reached 90 of the designated cards that they need. He hypes them up for their job well done, and the crowd cheers. Genthru speaks up, wanting to tell the group something, and reveals to them that he is the Bomber. He informs everyone that he has placed bombs on each of them and that he will trade each of their lives for a designated card.
